# Verify that `sesam init` installs a `git sesam` alias in the repo's local
# git config so users can drive sesam through git without any PATH plumbing.
# The shell prefix `!` tells git to run the value as a shell command instead
# of looking for a `git-sesam` binary on PATH.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Alias must be written to the local config (init never touches ~/.gitconfig).
exec git config --local alias.sesam
stdout '^!sesam$'

# `git sesam id` resolves through the alias and prints the current user.
exec git sesam id
stdout '^admin$'

# Subcommand routing works (multi-word verbs + secondary args).
exec git sesam ls
stdout 'README\.md'
stdout 'admin'

# A freshly-initialized repo passes `verify` (status lines go to stderr via slog).
exec git sesam verify
stderr 'Integrity: ok$'

# Extra flags pass through the alias intact.
exec git sesam ls --json
stdout '"RevealedPath": "README.md"'
